sections.label: Section 826
sections.translation: I did .

sections.label: Section 826b
sections.original: ὁρᾷς οὖν ὡς ἀγαθὸν τὸ μανθάνειν ;
sections.translation: Seest thou , then , how good a thing is learning ? There is no Jupiter , O Phidippides !

sections.label: Section 827b
sections.translation: Who then ?

sections.label: Section 828
sections.translation: Vortex reigns , having expelled Jupiter .

sections.label: Section 829
sections.translation: Bah ! Why do you talk foolishly ?

sections.label: Section 829b
sections.translation: Be assured that it is so .

sections.label: Section 830
sections.translation: Who says this ?

sections.label: Section 830b
sections.translation: Socrates the Melian , and Chaerephon , who knows the footmarks of fleas .

sections.label: Section 832
sections.original: σὺ δʼ ἐς τοσοῦτον τῶν μανιῶν ἐλήλυθας
sections.translation: Have you arrived at such a pitch of frenzy that you believe madmen ?

sections.label: Section 833b
sections.translation: Speak words of good omen , and say nothing bad of clever men

sections.label: Section 835
sections.original: καὶ νοῦν ἔχοντας · ὧν ὑπὸ τῆς φειδωλίας
sections.translation: and wise ; of whom , through frugality , none ever shaved or anointed himself , or went to a bath to wash himself ; while you squander my property in bathing , as if I were already dead . But go as quickly as possible and learn instead of me .

sections.label: Section 840
sections.original: τί δʼ ἂν παρʼ ἐκείνων καὶ μάθοι χρηστόν τις ἄν ;
sections.translation: What good could any one learn from them ?

sections.label: Section 841
sections.original: ἄληθες ; ὅσαπερ ἔστʼ ἐν ἀνθρώποις σοφά ·
sections.translation: What , really ? Whatever wisdom there is among men . And you will know yourself , how ignorant and stupid you are . But wait for me here a short time .

sections.label: Section 844
sections.original: οἴμοι τί δράσω παραφρονοῦντος τοῦ πατρός ;
sections.translation: Ah me ! What shall I do , my father being crazed ? Shall I bring him into court and convict him of lunacy , or shall I give information of his madness to the coffin-makers ?

sections.label: Section 847
sections.original: φέρʼ ἴδω , σὺ τοῦτον τί ὀνομάζεις ; εἰπέ μοι .
sections.translation: Come , let me see ; what do you consider this to be ? Tell me .

sections.label: Section 848
sections.translation: Alectryon .

sections.label: Section 848b
sections.translation: Right . And what this ?

sections.label: Section 849
sections.translation: Alectryon .

sections.label: Section 849b
sections.translation: Both the same ? You are very ridiculous . Do not do so , then , for the future ; but call this ἀλεκτρύαινα , and this one ἀλέκτωρ .

sections.label: Section 852
sections.translation: Ἀλεκτρύαινα ! Did you learn these clever things by going in just now to the Titans ?

sections.label: Section 854
sections.original: χἄτερά γε πόλλʼ · ἀλλʼ ὅ τι μάθοιμʼ ἑκάστοτε ,
sections.translation: And many others too ; but whatever I learned on each occasion I used to forget immediately , through length of years .

sections.label: Section 856
sections.original: διὰ ταῦτα δὴ καὶ θοἰμάτιον ἀπώλεσας ;
sections.translation: Is it for this reason , pray , that you have also lost your cloak ?

sections.label: Section 857
sections.translation: I have not lost it ; but have studied it away .

sections.label: Section 858
sections.original: τὰς δʼ ἐμβάδας ποῖ τέτροφας ὦνόητε σύ ;
sections.translation: What have you made of your slippers , you foolish man ?

sections.label: Section 859
sections.original: ὥσπερ Περικλέης ἐς τὸ δέον ἀπώλεσα .
sections.translation: I have expended them , like Pericles , for needful purposes .

sections.label: Section 860
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἴθι βάδιζʼ , ἴωμεν · εἶτα τῷ πατρὶ
sections.translation: Come , move , let us go . And then if you obey your father , go wrong if you like . I also know that I formerly obeyed you , a lisping child of six years old , and bought you a go-cart at the Diasia , with the first obolus I received from the Heliaea .

sections.label: Section 865
sections.original: ἦ μὴν σὺ τούτοις τῷ χρόνῳ ποτʼ ἀχθέσει .
sections.translation: You will assuredly some time at length be grieved at this .

sections.label: Section 866
sections.original: εὖ γʼ ὅτι ἐπείσθης . δεῦρο δεῦρʼ ὦ Σώκρατες ,
sections.translation: It is well done of you that you obeyed . Come hither , come hither O Socrates ! Come forth , for I bring to you this son of mine , having persuaded him against his will .

sections.label: Section 868b
sections.translation: For he is still childish , and not used to the baskets here .

sections.label: Section 870
sections.original: αὐτὸς τρίβων εἴης ἄν , εἰ κρέμαιό γε .
sections.translation: You would yourself be used to them if you were hanged .

sections.label: Section 871
sections.original: οὐκ ἐς κόρακας ; καταρᾷ σὺ τῷ διδασκάλῳ ;
sections.translation: A mischief take you ! Do you abuse your teacher ?

sections.label: Section 872
sections.translation: Were hanged quoth ’ a ! How sillily he pronounced it , and with lips wide apart ! How can this youth ever learn an acquittal from a trial

sections.label: Section 875
sections.translation: or a legal summons , or persuasive refutation ? And yet Hyperbolus learned this at the cost of a talent .

sections.label: Section 877
sections.translation: Never mind ; teach him . He is clever by nature . Indeed , from his earliest years , when he was a little fellow only so big , he was wont to form houses and carve ships within-doors ,

sections.label: Section 880
sections.translation: and make little wagons of leather , and make frogs out of pomegranate-rinds , you can ’ t think how cleverly . But see that he learns those two causes ; the better , whatever it may be ; and the worse , which , by maintaining what is unjust , overturns the better .

sections.label: Section 885
sections.original: ἐὰν δὲ μή , τὸν γοῦν ἄδικον πάσῃ τέχνῃ .
sections.translation: If not both , at any rate the unjust one by all means .

sections.label: Section 886
sections.original: αὐτὸς μαθήσεται παρʼ αὐτοῖν τοῖν λόγοιν .
sections.translation: He shall learn it himself from the two causes in person .

sections.label: Section 887b
sections.translation: I will take my departure . Remember this now , that he is to be able to reply to all just arguments .

sections.label: Section 889
sections.translation: Come hither ! Show yourself to the spectators , although being audacious .

sections.label: Section 891
sections.original: ἴθʼ ὅποι χρῄζεις . πολὺ γὰρ μᾶλλόν ʼς
sections.translation: Go whither you please ; for I shall far rather do for you , if I speak before a crowd .

sections.label: Section 893
sections.translation: You destroy me ? Who are you ?

sections.label: Section 893b
sections.translation: A cause .

sections.label: Section 893c
sections.translation: Ay , the worse .

sections.label: Section 894
sections.original: ἀλλά σε νικῶ τὸν ἐμοῦ κρείττω
sections.translation: But I conquer you , who say that you are better than I .

sections.label: Section 895b
sections.translation: By doing what clever trick ?

sections.label: Section 896
sections.translation: By discovering new contrivances .

sections.label: Section 897
sections.translation: For these innovations flourish by the favour of these silly persons .

sections.label: Section 899
sections.translation: No ; but wise persons .

sections.label: Section 899b
sections.translation: I will destroy you miserably .

sections.label: Section 900
sections.translation: Tell me , by doing what ?

sections.label: Section 900b
sections.translation: By speaking what is just .

sections.label: Section 901
sections.translation: But I will overturn them by contradicting them ; for I deny that justice even exists at all .

sections.label: Section 903
sections.translation: Do you deny that it exists ?

sections.label: Section 903b
sections.translation: For come , where is it ?

sections.label: Section 904
sections.translation: With the gods .

sections.label: Section 904a
sections.original: πῶς δῆτα δίκης οὔσης ὁ Ζεὺς
sections.translation: How , then , if justice exists , has Jupiter not perished , who bound his own father ?

sections.label: Section 906b
sections.translation: Bah ! This profanity now is spreading ! Give me a basin .

sections.label: Section 908
sections.translation: You are a dotard and absurd .

sections.label: Section 909
sections.translation: You are debauched and shameless .

sections.label: Section 910
sections.translation: You have spoken roses of me .
